Carcinoma_in_situ.immediate_action: Urgent treatment including possible surgery or radiotherapy.
Carcinoma_in_situ.lifestyle_changes: Avoid smoking, maintain a healthy diet and follow medical advice.
Carcinoma_in_situ.follow_up: Close monitoring including frequent Pap smears and clinical exams.

normal_intermediate.immediate_action: Regular screening and evaluation by specialist.
normal_intermediate.follow_up: Pap smear every 6-12 months based on physician recommendation.

normal_superficiel.immediate_action: Routine monitoring as this is often a normal cell type.
treatment_options: No treatment required unless abnormal changes detected.

normal_columnar.lifestyle_changes: Avoid smoking and exposure to HPV.
normal_columnar.follow_up: Follow-up Pap smears as per guidelines.

light_dysplastic.immediate_action: Monitor with Pap smears every 6 months with possible colposcopy.

moderate_dysplastic.lifestyle_changes: Avoid smoking, reduce exposure to HPV and other irritants.

severe_dysplastic.immediate_action: Immediate intervention with colposcopy and biopsy, possible treatment.
severe_dysplastic.follow_up: Repeat Pap smear and evaluation every 3 months post treatment.
